titleStmt.title: Letter from J. F. Henderson to Matthew Nathan, 28 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. F. Henderson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from J. F. Henderson assistant secretary in the Home Office to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the under-secretary to the chief secretary of Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. In the letter the men are discussing a case involving F. G. Kitts.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7621/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE,
WHITEHALL. 28th April, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to refer to
his letter of the 10th instant, in the case of Mr. F.G.
Kitts, and to say that Mr Kitts is believed by his mother
to be residing at 69, Kenilworth Square, Rathgar: she
states that she has not seen or heard from him for 4 months. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, J. F. Henderson The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Chief Comms
D.M.P.
Please state
Mr Kitts' present address
EOF
8.V.16 Supt. E Div. Sergt Megahey
for report
O'Kiernan Supt. 19/5/16 Castle Reg.
W.A.M. 9/5 First rep. not yet
recd. in Cen. Reg.
J.C. 18/5/16
